title: Fix confined no-write-down bypass (can_write + eval_cap)
description: AUDIT #4 HIGH. writes_down uses global .all() over every clearance and ignores scope, so any second write clearance not strictly dominating the target flips deny->allow. Base decision on in-scope write-capable clearances whose ceiling equals the label; mirror in eval_cap; multi-clearance/scoped regression tests. File: src/acl/mod.rs:273-292,355-373

title: Wire policy engine into mutation paths + signed-approval resolver
description: AUDIT #3 HIGH. evaluate_replayable has no caller; advance_timeline/merge_timelines/apply_push_ops never run hooks; SignedApprovalHook unreachable from resolve_hook. Run deterministic registry on Advance, at merge-apply with real result head, and per accepted RefUpdateOp; add 'signed-approval' resolver arm; make hook gate Advance (or model merge-commit as Merge); deprecate forgeable ref-counting ApprovalHook. Files: src/repo/mod.rs, src/sync/session.rs, src/acl/hook.rs, src/acl/attestation.rs

title: serve_fetch: enforce read-ACL on served objects, not just advert
description: AUDIT #2 HIGH. serve_fetch passes client-supplied want raw to missing_closure/build_pack with no ACL re-check -> ref-granularity read control bypassed; read revocation ineffective. Intersect wants with closure reachable from accessor's authorized adverts, as the HTTP path already does. File: src/sync/session.rs:39-52

title: Bound decompression + wire buffering (zstd bomb, frame/header/body caps)
description: AUDIT #1 HIGH. decode_pack uses zstd::decode_all with no output cap (pre-auth OOM); deframe/TcpConn::recv/read_http buffer unbounded; O(n^2) find rescan. Cap per-object/per-frame/total-pack/frame-length-prefix/header-bytes/Content-Length; streaming decode aborting past declared ulen; fix find rescan. Files: src/store/pack.rs, src/sync/{transport,wire,http}.rs

title: Fix cargo-audit advisories in gix transitive deps
description: cargo audit: RUSTSEC-2025-0021 (gix-features 0.40.0, SHA-1 collision detection missing, medium 6.8) and RUSTSEC-2025-0140 (gix-date 0.9.4, non-utf8 TimeBuf::as_str) — both transitive via gix 0.70 (git import/export paths). Plus unmaintained warnings atomic-polyfill (RUSTSEC-2023-0089), proc-macro-error2 (RUSTSEC-2026-0173). Bump gix to a version pulling gix-features>=0.41.0 and gix-date>=0.12.0; re-run cargo audit.

title: Replace glob_matches with linear/DP matcher + load-time limits
description: AUDIT #8 MED. Catastrophic backtracking (ReDoS) on attacker-influenced patterns/paths. Memoize on (pat_idx,s_idx) or iterative two-pointer; cap pattern/path length and ** count; reject pathological patterns at policy load. File: src/acl/glob.rs

title: Threat model doc + unaudited README caveat
description: AUDIT #6 HIGH (fix-item F). docs/THREAT_MODEL.md stating adversary/scope; README 'experimental, unaudited, do not expose a network listener' caveat. Container for latent scaffold risks (unauth transports, unloaded lattices, dead signed-ref/authn code).

title: advance_timeline: write-check path deletions (symmetric diff)
description: AUDIT #7 MED. Only new tree is walked, so removing a protected path the actor cannot write is uncaught. Diff old head vs new snapshot; check can_write over symmetric difference. File: src/repo/mod.rs:508-574

title: Adversarial/negative test suite for verify + enforcement
description: AUDIT #5 HIGH (fix-item C). Negative tests for verify_chain/verify_attestation/count_valid_approvals, multi-clearance/scoped can_write, and end-to-end protected-merge-denied-without-N-attestations. Acceptance evidence for the enforcement fixes.

title: explain_path: decision-trace API for access decisions
description: Surface the existing lattice access logic as repo.explain_path(actor, snapshot, path) -> decision trace (allow/deny + reason chain: label_for_path, dominance, confined no-write-down, matched rule). Attacks the lattice-usability risk. Logic already exists in can_read/can_write; just needs a trace-producing wrapper + CLI verb.

title: WS5 follow-up: policy authority (TrustStore + PolicyVerifier + signed chain)
description: Deferred from WS5 (bole-cy6). Add sync::authority: TrustStore (per-repo trusted signing keys), PolicyVerifier (verify offered PolicyRoot chain to a TrustAnchor-signed root, fail-closed on unsigned/untrusted/unknown-hook/broken-chain), highest-rooted-wins conflict resolution, PolicyOffer transfer + TOFU on clone. Fills WS1's reserved Grant.signature semantics. Supersedes/subsumes bole-fz1 (signed approval attestation). See spec ws5 section 5. O2 direct-anchor v1.
close_reason: Policy authority complete (WS5 5). Ed25519 detached signatures (ed25519-dalek), TrustStore/TrustAnchor, PolicySigner (seed/KMS-swappable), verify_chain (fail-closed: present + lattice/rules present + hooks resolvable + direct-anchor signed), reconcile (highest-rooted-wins / Diverged). Full workspace green: 22 binaries, exit 0, clippy clean. 3 tests (sign+verify, unsigned/untrusted/bad-hook reject, longer-chain-wins/divergent). Unblocks bole-fz1. Commit 6b88795.

title: WS5 follow-up: wire codec + Transport trait + HTTP/SSH transports
description: Deferred from WS5 (bole-cy6). Add sync::wire (Message enum + length-prefixed postcard frame codec + CapSet/version handshake), sync::transport (Transport/Conn trait, InProcessTransport, HttpTransport v1, SshTransport), and sync::session SyncSession state machine driving fetch/push over the trait. Makes the in-process core engine genuinely transport-agnostic + networked. See spec 2026-06-29-ws5 sections 3,7. O1: HTTP first.
close_reason: Sync wire codec + Transport/Conn trait + InProcessConn + SyncSession state machine (serve/client_fetch/client_push) complete. Protocol driven over any Conn, reusing WS4 packs + RefTransaction CAS + WS1 authz. Full workspace green: 22 binaries, exit 0, clippy clean. 5 new tests (3 wire, 2 session over InProcess). No new deps. Concrete TCP/HTTP/SSH transports = bole-vih. Commits f746431.

title: WS4 follow-up: atomic ref transactions (RefTransaction + journal)
description: Deferred from WS4 (bole-81z). Add Repository::transaction() -> RefTransaction with buffered create_tag/move_tag/create_timeline/advance_head/delete_ref + CAS preconditions (expect, advance_head_if), committing all-or-nothing via a write-ahead journal over the existing per-file ref layout (refs/.txn/<txid>.journal recording absolute final ref values; commit point = journal fsync; idempotent replay on open()). Repo lock refs/.txn/lock serializes commits and excludes GC planning/retire. See spec 2026-06-29-ws4 section 8. Decision: journal-over-per-file (not packed-refs).
close_reason: Atomic multi-ref transactions complete. RefTransaction builder (create_tag/move_tag/create_timeline/advance_head/delete_ref + expect/advance_head_if CAS), all-or-nothing commit via absolute-value plan, journal-over-per-file on disk (fsync commit point + idempotent replay on open), Error::TransactionConflict. Full workspace green: 21 binaries, exit 0, clippy clean. Unblocks WS5 CAS-on-heads. Commits 43500d5.

title: WS4: storage at scale (packs + index + GC + atomic refs)
description: Pack format (designed as the wire payload for WS5), repack, mark-sweep GC, atomic multi-ref transactions. See spec 2026-06-29-ws4.
close_reason: WS4 storage-at-scale complete (scope: pack format + PackedDiskBackend + repack + mark-sweep GC + count/compact; atomic ref transactions deferred to bole-sk6). Pack .pack/.idx self-verifying (WS5 wire payload), loose-first packed backend, crash-safe repack, GC from ref roots + extra_roots (registry-safe) with grace window, bole store repack/gc CLI. Full workspace green: 21 test binaries, exit 0, clippy clean. Decisions: plans/2026-07-01-ws4-decisions.md. Commits 4a15426..cd0de39.

title: WS5: distributed (remote/fetch/push, sync protocol)
description: Transport-agnostic sync over content-addressed packs; CAS on timeline heads; policy/label authority model. Needs WS1+WS4. See spec 2026-06-29-ws5.
close_reason: WS5 core sync engine complete (scope: in-process fetch/push/clone + CAS; wire codec/Transport/HTTP=bole-6qy, policy verification=bole-0tp, authn=bole-6h7 deferred). Negotiated missing-closure transfer via self-verifying WS4 packs, remote-tracking refs, git-model CAS push (expected_old=tracking ref) FF/policy gated, clone. Full workspace green: 22 test binaries, exit 0, clippy clean. Decisions: plans/2026-07-01-ws5-decisions.md. Commits 0d27e62.

title: WS1: hybrid access/policy core (labels + clearances + hook)
description: Replace glob-ACL 'lattice' with a real hybrid model: label lattice foundation, glob ACLs as a degenerate case, programmable PolicyHook. Unifies actor/acl/accessor into one pipeline. See spec 2026-06-29-ws1.
close_reason: WS1 hybrid access/policy core complete: 10/10 TDD tasks. Label lattice + rules + clearances + Accessor rewrite + Object::Policy + AclBackend shims + PolicyHook/registry + repo integration + ApprovalHook. All 247 prior tests preserved; full suite 191 lib + integration green. Commits 4576e7f..e8af940.

title: fix(repo): check_merge resolve snapshot to tree root
description: check_merge passes source_head (snapshot ObjectId) directly to walk_tree_filtered which expects a tree ObjectId. Must resolve snapshot to get snap.root before walking.

title: Gate 3 fix: check_merge always-Allowed bug + bead tags
description: check_merge passes empty Accessor to walk_tree_filtered so protected paths are never collected. Fix by walking with privileged all-paths accessor. Also fix retroactive bole-1vi tag and missing bole-9by on lib.rs.

title: Gate 1 fixes: blocking exists, tokio features, atomic writes
description: Three fixes from final code review: (1) DiskBackend::exists uses blocking Path::exists() — replace with tokio::fs::try_exists; (2) Cargo.toml has tokio features=full in dependencies — replace with minimal features; (3) DiskBackend::put is non-atomic on crash — use write-then-rename pattern

title: Wire signed-approval hook: resolver arm + approver/attestation persistence
description: Follow-up to bole-rdh. Add a 'signed-approval' resolve_hook arm backed by a persistent ApproverRegistry + head-bound Attestation store loaded from the repo (design: where approvers/attestations live — objects vs refs vs CLI registry; likely SignedApprovalHook.check loads lazily from PolicyContext). Then deprecate/remove the forgeable ref-counting ApprovalHook. Until then, approvals are guarded by the THREAT_MODEL 'do not rely on approvals' caveat. Depends on bole-rdh, bole-0wr.

title: Domain-separate + repo-bind all Ed25519 signed payloads
description: AUDIT #11 LOW (fix-item A). Frame policy-root, attestation, ref-op messages with per-scheme constant tag + repo/namespace id; verify same. Prevents cross-repo/cross-scheme signature replay with shared keys. Files: src/acl/authority.rs, src/acl/attestation.rs, src/sync/authn.rs

title: Push-create: honor real TimelinePolicy + validate new_head
description: AUDIT #10 LOW. Push-created timelines hardcode Unrestricted (permanent history-rewrite) and never verify new_head decodes to a Snapshot. Carry source policy (or default FastForwardOnly); validate head object. Files: src/sync/session.rs, src/sync/mod.rs

title: Authorize push before landing objects + per-connection quotas
description: AUDIT #9 LOW. Both push responders put_raw every object before authz/FF check. Quarantine received objects, promote only on accepted ref ops; enforce size/count quotas. Files: src/sync/session.rs, src/sync/http.rs

title: Spec + enforce PolicyHook determinism contract
description: Hooks are async with objects/refs/now in scope; nothing enforces purity/replayability. Under CAS-on-heads sync two nodes can diverge on a non-deterministic hook. Spec the contract (pure fn of object graph + event) and add guardrails where cheap (drop wall-clock now / make it event-supplied).

title: Monotonic-visibility property test for ACL filtering
description: Pin the invariant that filtering only removes visibility: never changes object identity/hashes, never reorders trees, never synthesizes nodes. Property test: filtered visible_paths is a subset of full paths and every retained entry has identical ObjectId to the unfiltered tree.

title: docs: bring README/CLI/API current with shipped follow-ups
description: After bole-3hj (README) and bole-9c0 (CLI/API/skills), several workstreams shipped: bole-vih (TCP/HTTP transports), bole-0tp (policy authority), bole-6h7 (authn), bole-fz1 (signed approvals), bole-vw9 (KMS), bole-58u (git import CLI). Update: (a) README Status/roadmap table + positioning to mark these realized (honesty rule); (b) docs/CLI.md add git import, store repack/gc, env resolve, bole run, secret rekey; (c) docs/API.md add reference sections for packs/PackedDiskBackend, envelope secrets, git_import, policy authority, attestations, RefTransaction, sync.
close_reason: Docs brought current: README roadmap table/positioning (all shipped follow-ups marked realized, honesty rule restored), CLI.md +6 commands (secret rekey/env resolve/run/git import/store repack+gc), API.md +Extended API section covering the new library surface, skills critical facts updated + in sync. Doc-only. Commit.

title: WS5 follow-up: concrete network transports (TCP/HTTP/SSH)
description: Deferred from bole-6qy. Add concrete Transport/Conn impls over the network: a framed TCP transport (tokio net feature + the wire length-prefix frame codec), then HTTP(S) per spec 7.2 (POST /bole/v1/{info,fetch,push}) and SSH (bole sync-serve --stdio). The wire codec, Transport/Conn trait, InProcessTransport, and SyncSession state machine already exist from bole-6qy; this bead only adds concrete transports + streaming pack bodies + resumability. See spec 2026-06-29-ws5 section 7.
close_reason: Concrete network transports: TCP (TcpConn reuses SyncSession over a framed socket + serve_tcp_once) and minimal HTTP/1.1 (POST /bole/v1/{fetch,push}, single request per verb, no framework). Real over-the-socket fetch/push, loopback tests for both. Full workspace green: 22 binaries, exit 0, clippy clean. Added tokio net feature. SSH + TLS/proxy-grade HTTP deferred. Commits 1e8a4d6, 961eb60.

title: WS6 follow-up: bole git import CLI verb (WS7 ergonomics)
description: Deferred from WS6 (bole-mtq). Add the 'bole git import <path>' CLI verb wrapping repo::git_import::git_import: --branch <name> (repeatable), --timeline-policy ff|append|unrestricted, --label-ruleset <file>, --dry-run, --force (non-FF re-import). Output one line per timeline/tag + summary. Remote URL support (fetch+import) also a WS7 concern. See spec 2026-06-29-ws6 section 8.2.
close_reason: bole git import CLI verb complete (wraps git_import: --branch/--timeline-policy/--label-ruleset/--dry-run/--force). Round-trip CLI test. Full workspace green: 23 binaries, clippy clean. Commit.

title: WS5 follow-up: authn/authz (PeerIdentity -> Actor -> Accessor) + signed refs
description: Deferred from WS5 (bole-cy6). Add sync::authn: PeerIdentity/Principal, ActorMap (principal->actor-name), Accessor construction from the actor's ClearanceGrant; server-side push authz reuses WS1 can_write_timeline/can_write_path/PolicyHook. Anonymous->public-read mapping. Optional SIGNED_REFS: per-RefOp signature over (name,expected_old,new_head). See spec ws5 section 6.
close_reason: Sync authn/authz complete (WS5 6). ActorMap (principal->actor) + accessor_for building the WS1 Accessor from the mapped actor's ClearanceGrant (anonymous->empty/public-read-no-write); no second authz model. RefSigner/verify_ref_op ed25519 signed-ref helpers. MemoryAclBackend grant storage + AclStore::set_grant. End-to-end: principal->accessor->authorized push over a session, anonymous denied. Full workspace green: 22 binaries, exit 0, clippy clean. 3 tests. Commit 5daafc5.

title: DiskAclBackend: persist secret ACLs (parity with Memory)
description: WS3 (bole-9mz) added secret-ACL storage as default-empty AclBackend trait methods (list/set/delete_secret_acl) and overrode them in MemoryAclBackend. DiskAclBackend still uses the default no-op, so secret protection rules do not persist to disk. Implement file-backed secret ACL storage in src/acl/disk.rs mirroring the path/timeline ACL pattern, so resolve_overlay access checks work on disk repos too.
close_reason: DiskAclBackend secret-ACL persistence complete (acls/secrets/ postcard files, set/delete/list overrides, projects into ruleset). Persist-across-reopen test. Full workspace green: 23 binaries, clippy clean. Commit.

title: WS3 follow-up: KmsKeyProvider + KmsClient trait (feature-gated)
description: Deferred from WS3 (bole-9mz). Add KmsKeyProvider implementing the KeyProvider trait by delegating wrap_dk/unwrap_dk to an injected KmsClient trait (operator-provided: AWS KMS, Vault Transit, PKCS#11 HSM). Ship the KmsClient trait shape (encrypt/decrypt of <=4KB blobs) + one reference adapter behind a feature flag; do not embed a KMS. WrappedKey.key_ref carries the KMS key ARN+version. Also add the --kms <uri> CLI flag and a fake KmsClient for tests. See spec 2026-06-29-ws3-secrets-env O7.
close_reason: KMS slot complete (feature kms): KmsClient trait + KmsKeyProvider (delegates DK wrap/unwrap) + LocalKmsClient reference adapter. Envelope round-trip + wrong-key tests. Full workspace green default AND --features kms: 23 binaries, clippy clean. Real cloud adapters are third-party KmsClient impls. Commit.

title: Replace placeholder approval refs with signed attestation format
description: WS1 (bole-fo2) records merge approvals as placeholder refs under refs/approval/<target>/<approver>, tallied by acl::hook::count_approvals. Replace with the real signed-attestation format. This is WS5/O4 territory (see spec 2026-06-29-ws5-distributed-sync). The PolicyHook/ApprovalHook interface should not need to change.
design: Keep ApprovalHook trait surface stable; swap count_approvals + approval_ref_prefix storage for signed attestations. Depends on WS5 sync/identity work.
close_reason: Signed approval attestations complete. Attestation (ed25519 over target+head), ApproverRegistry, AttestationSigner, verify_attestation, count_valid_approvals (distinct registered approvers, exact head), SignedApprovalHook. Placeholder approval refs marked superseded (kept for HookSpec 'approval' kind). Full workspace green: 22 binaries, exit 0, clippy clean. 2 tests. Commit 05e2e10.

Staleness classifier + workspace prune/repair(R1/R2/R3)/list --check with git-like exit codes; 8 integration tests. Decisions: plans/2026-07-01-ws7-decisions.md (NOTE: not written - decisions inline in commits). Commits e6ed0f2, 3c068a1.

description: git_import via gix mirroring export; identity map for round-trip; default label assignment. Needs WS1. See spec 2026-06-29-ws6.

title: WS3: complete secrets/env (resolve+run, envelope keys, rekey)
description: env resolve / run --env, KeyProvider trait, envelope encryption (per-secret data key wrapped by master), secret rekey. See spec 2026-06-29-ws3.

KeyProvider/ProviderChain, SecretV2 envelope (postcard deviation: new Object::SecretV2 not untagged enum), resolve_overlay (WS1-gated fail-closed), rekey (rewrap v2 / upgrade v1, auto-repoint registry+overlays), secret ACLs (Memory; Disk parity=bole-6l2), CLI env resolve/run + secret rekey. Full workspace green: 21 test binaries, exit 0. Decisions: plans/2026-07-01-ws3-decisions.md. Commits 504cdf6..049c402.

title: WS2: unify workspace model (Workspace trait: disk + memory)
description: Extract one Workspace trait with DiskWorkspace and in-memory impls so the CLI worktree and EphemeralWorkspace are one model. See spec 2026-06-29-ws2.
close_reason: WS2 workspace unification complete. Workspace trait (async_trait) + EphemeralWorkspace impl (additive) + DiskWorkspace (filesystem-backed, single disk-walk excluding .bole/symlinks, write-through, lazy). CLI snapshot create / workspace diff / workspace show retrofitted onto DiskWorkspace; duplicate collect_blobs removed. Also fixed WS1 fallout (Object::Policy CLI match). Full workspace green: lib 197 + cli 38. Decisions doc: plans/2026-07-01-ws2-decisions.md. Commits 18f49fb..92fa0c3.

title: Library: EphemeralWorkspace in-memory worktree API
description: Add a pure in-RAM worktree for agents/tools. New src/repo/ephemeral.rs with: reusable primitives build_tree(objects,&map), snapshot_paths(objects,snap), diff_paths(base,target)->PathDiff; EphemeralWorkspace<'a> borrowing &Repository with read/write/remove/paths/diff/commit/base; Repository::ephemeral_workspace() (empty) and ephemeral_workspace_from(snap) (seeded). commit builds a tree from in-memory files and stores a snapshot with parents=base (does NOT auto-advance a timeline). No ACL filtering in MVP. diff/commit may store blobs. created_at passed in. Export from lib.rs. Refactor bole-cli/src/worktree.rs to delegate build_root_tree/snapshot_blobs/diff to the lifted library primitives. No behavior change to existing commands.
acceptance_criteria: EphemeralWorkspace round-trips: write/commit/seed-from-snapshot/diff/remove work in memory; commit parents chain to base; CLI worktree.rs delegates to library primitives; existing CLI + lib tests still pass; new lib unit tests cover empty+seeded commit, diff add/remove/modify, and parent chaining.

title: CLI: linked worktrees (workspace add/list/remove)
description: Multi-worktree support sharing one .bole/ store. A linked worktree is a directory with a .bole FILE ({store,id}) pointing at the primary store; its binding lives in .bole/worktrees/<id>/state.json, registered in .bole/worktrees.json. Discovery in context.rs handles .bole-dir (primary, cli-state.json) vs .bole-file (linked). New commands: workspace add <path> --timeline <name> [--as actor] (timeline must exist; materialize head), workspace list (primary + linked: path/timeline/head), workspace remove <path> (unregister + delete pointer/metadata, never user files). Warn-but-allow binding a timeline already used by another worktree. Backward compatible with existing single-worktree repos.
acceptance_criteria: workspace add creates a linked dir bound to a timeline with the head materialized; commands run from it resolve the shared store and its own binding; workspace list shows primary+linked; workspace remove cleans metadata without deleting files; existing repos unaffected; integration tests cover add/list/remove + isolation of bindings.

title: Update all documentation to match current library + CLI
description: docs/API.md predates API reconciliations and is stale: EntryKind (only Blob/Tree), EnvValue (Plain/Secret), put_overlay/get_overlay (not put_env/get_env), Object::EnvOverlay, Timeline fields (head/policy/created_at/kind/expires_at), create_timeline signature, TimelinePolicy variants (FastForwardOnly/Append/Unrestricted), and the complete example uses the invalid EntryKind::Secret. Also add Error::PolicyViolation, document TimelinePolicy enforcement (bole-3w9), correct glob semantics (bole-l54: ** across segments incl. mid-pattern, * single segment, case-sensitive, src/** is descendants-only). Update README.md, docs/CLI.md, and bole-cli/README.md to note policy enforcement and glob semantics.

title: Full ACL lattice test coverage
description: Close ACL-lattice test gaps: (1) exhaustive glob_matches suite (mid-path *, partial-segment *, ** in middle, case sensitivity, zero-width *, empty/long edge cases, bare-prefix dir); (2) Accessor/lattice invariants in acl/mod.rs (privileged() is read-only on paths AND timelines, read_role_does_not_grant_write, write-only role cannot read a protected path, positive can_write_path/can_write_timeline, role union); (3) merge_timelines AccessDenied on a non-writable target. Library tests only; surface any real glob bug found.
acceptance_criteria: New tests added in src/acl/glob.rs, src/acl/mod.rs, and a repo-level merge-denial test. Any bug found in glob_matches is fixed. Full workspace suite passes.

title: Enforce TimelinePolicy in advance_timeline
description: Repository::advance_timeline (src/repo/mod.rs ~line 279) checks timeline and path write ACLs but never consults the timeline's TimelinePolicy before calling refs.advance_head. As a result FastForwardOnly and Append are stored but not enforced: a timeline created with --policy ff can be advanced to a non-descendant snapshot. The CLI faithfully stores the policy (bole-cli), so the gap is purely in the library.
design:
Load the target Timeline, read its policy, and gate the advance on the new head's relationship to the current head BEFORE refs.advance_head:
- FastForwardOnly: reject unless current head is an ancestor of new head (reuse the existing LCA/ancestry helper used by merge, e.g. find_common_ancestor: new head is a fast-forward iff lca(current, new) == current).
- Append: reject unless current head is an ancestor of new head (same descendant check; arbitrary rewrites disallowed). Distinguish from ff only if/when a weaker rule is defined; otherwise treat both as 'must descend from current head'.
- Unrestricted: no ancestry constraint (current behaviour).
Return Error::WrongRefKind or a new Error variant (e.g. Error::PolicyViolation) with a clear message. Decide whether advance_head (raw RefStore setter) stays policy-free (it should: it is the low-level primitive; enforcement lives in the ACL/policy-aware Repository layer).
acceptance_criteria: 1. advance_timeline on a ff/append timeline returns an error when the new head is not a descendant of the current head. 2. advance_timeline on a ff/append timeline succeeds for a true fast-forward. 3. Unrestricted timelines are unaffected. 4. Unit tests in src/repo cover ff-reject, ff-accept, append-reject, and unrestricted-allow. 5. Existing 222 workspace tests still pass; CLI timeline advance still works for unrestricted (its default).

title: CLI: full integration-test coverage
description: Add tests for every untested leaf subcommand and key flag/verdict: tag list/delete, snapshot show/--author, workspace show/clear/--as, actor list/current, acl timeline + can-read-*, secret rotate, object get, merge requires-approval/rejected verdicts, timeline --policy storage, --quiet, branch alias, and error paths.

title: CLI: secret + env command groups
description: Add name->id registries (.bole/secrets.json, .bole/envs.json) and key resolution (64-hex via --key-env/--key-file). secret put/reveal/rotate/list wrap put_secret/get_secret; env create/set/set-secret/show/list wrap put_overlay/get_overlay (overlays are immutable, so edits create new objects and update the registry). env show redacts secret-backed values. No src/ changes.

title: CLI: merge + git export commands
description: Add merge (check via check_merge; run via merge_timelines, building a merged snapshot and advancing the target when clean, reporting conflicts otherwise) and git export (project_to_git) command groups. No src/ changes.

title: CLI: actor registry + acl commands
description: Add a named-actor registry persisted at .bole/actors.json, an effective_accessor helper that builds an Accessor from the bound actor (falling back to full access), and the actor (create/list/show/grant-path/grant-timeline/use) and acl (path|timeline protect/list/unprotect, can-* checks) command groups. Wire effective_accessor into timeline advance and snapshot create. No src/ changes.

title: CLI: workspace + snapshot commands
description: Add work-tree<->tree differ (worktree.rs) and the workspace (open/show/materialize/diff) and snapshot (create --from-workspace/show/list/parents/diff) command groups. Wraps ObjectStore, materialize, get_snapshot_filtered. No src/ changes.

title: CLI: resolver + timeline + tag commands
description: Add shared @-shortcut/ref/hex resolver and the timeline (list/create/show/advance/delete + branch aliases) and tag (create/list/show/delete) command groups, wrapping RefStore and Repository::advance_timeline. No src/ changes.

title: Scaffold cargo workspace + bole-cli crate
description: Convert repo to a cargo workspace. Keep bole lib unchanged. Add bole-cli member with bin 'bole', clap arg parsing, and a compiling skeleton (init + status stubs). No changes to src/.

title: API reference markdown document
description: Write docs/API.md — a single user-readable markdown document covering all public bole API types, methods, and usage patterns. No code reading required; written for a developer who wants to understand how to use the library without reading source.

title: Add rustdoc API documentation
description: Add /// doc comments to all public API items in src/: ObjectId, Object, Blob, Snapshot, Tree, TreeEntry, EntryKind, Secret, EnvOverlay, EnvValue, Repository and its methods, ObjectStore, Accessor, PathRole, TimelineRole, Permission, RefName, Ref, TimelinePolicy, MergeCheck, FilteredSnapshot, Error, ParseObjectIdError, and public free functions.

title: Add README.md
description: Write a README.md for the bole crate covering what it is, key concepts, quick start example, architecture overview, and feature status through Gate 8.

title: G8-T2: T8 storage deduplication tests
description: Add tests/scale.rs with two sub-tests: t8_object_count_dedup (MemoryBackend, 1000 snapshots, verifies unique object count ≤ naive/3) and t8_disk_storage_footprint (DiskBackend, 100 snapshots, verifies unique object count ≤ naive/3 on disk).

title: G8-T1: criterion benchmark scaffold
description: Add criterion 0.5 to dev-dependencies, three [[bench]] entries to Cargo.toml, and three bench files: benches/object_store.rs (put/get benchmarks), benches/snapshot_ops.rs (snapshot/timeline/merge benchmarks), benches/git_projection.rs (project_to_git benchmarks).

title: G7: project_to_git missing idempotent-open and not-a-repo error
description: project_to_git calls gix::init_bare unconditionally. Spec mandates try-open fallback: if target_path already contains a valid bare repo, open it (idempotent re-run). If it exists but is not a git repo, return Err(GitProjection). Currently neither contract is implemented or tested.

title: G7-T3: T7 integration tests
description: Five T7 integration tests verifying git projection from the library's public API

title: G7-T2: implement project_to_git
description: Five-pass git projection: init, topo-sort, write objects, write branch refs, write tag refs

title: G7-T1: git projection scaffolding
description: Add gix dep, GitProjection error variant, stub project_to_git, wire module

title: Gate 6 T4: T6 integration tests
description: Create tests/multi_actor.rs with t6_merge_non_conflicting, t6_merge_conflict, t6_agent_capability_enforced, t6_ephemeral_prune integration tests.

title: Gate 6 T3: Repository merge_timelines, advance_timeline, prune_timeline, find_common_ancestor
description: Add four methods to impl Repository: find_common_ancestor delegates to merge::lca; merge_timelines does write-cap check + LCA + three-way diff; advance_timeline enforces write caps on timeline and paths; prune_timeline removes expired timelines with no tags on head.

title: Gate 6 T2: MergeConflict, MergeResult, three_way_diff, find_common_ancestor
description: Create src/repo/merge.rs with pure merge logic. MergeConflict+MergeResult types, three_way_diff pure fn over BTreeMaps, find_common_ancestor async BFS. Add pub mod merge to repo/mod.rs. Re-export from lib.rs.

title: Gate 6 T1: Timeline kind+expires_at fields, Accessor::privileged
description: Add kind:String and expires_at:Option<u64> to Timeline struct (serde-defaulted). Update create_timeline signature. Add Accessor::privileged() constructor. Fix all existing callsites in src/ and tests/.

title: Gate 4 T4: T4 integration tests
description: Create tests/secrets.rs with t4_secret_roundtrip, t4_workspace_view, t4_workspace_view_acl_filtered integration tests.

title: Gate 4 T3: WorkspaceView and compute_workspace_view
description: Create WorkspaceView struct. Implement Repository::compute_workspace_view: get_snapshot_filtered for ACL-filtered files, get_overlay for env map, resolve Plain values directly and Secret values via get_secret+UTF-8.

title: Gate 4 T2: ObjectStore put_secret/get_secret/put_overlay/get_overlay
description: Add four convenience methods to ObjectStore: put_secret encrypts and stores, get_secret fetches and decrypts, put_overlay stores postcard-encoded EnvOverlay, get_overlay fetches and decodes.

title: Gate 4 T1: Secret, EnvOverlay types, error variants, Cargo deps
description: Add chacha20poly1305+rand deps. Secret struct with encrypt/decrypt (caller-supplied key, random nonce). EnvValue+EnvOverlay types. Object::Secret+Object::EnvOverlay variants. DecryptionFailed+SecretNotUtf8 error variants. lib.rs re-exports.

title: Gate 3 T4: T3 integration tests
description: Create tests/acl.rs with three T3 spec tests: path filtering (3-path snapshot with ACL, filtered by empty/partial/full accessor), timeline filtering (list_refs_filtered hides protected timelines), merge_check (RequiresApproval and Rejected variants).

title: Gate 3 T3: Repository filtered methods
description: Add acls: AclStore to Repository, update memory()/disk() constructors. Add FilteredSnapshot, MergeCheck types. Implement get_snapshot_filtered (recursive tree walk with ACL filtering), list_refs_filtered, and check_merge. Wire new types in lib.rs.

title: Gate 3 T2: DiskAclBackend
description: Full DiskAclBackend implementation: files under <root>/acls/paths/ and <root>/acls/timelines/, postcard encoding, glob-to-filename sanitization (%2F/%2A), atomic leading-dot temp writes, persist across reopen.

title: Gate 3 T1 fix: AclStore tests + Eq derives
description: Add AclStore integration tests covering path_is_protected and timeline_is_protected. Derive Eq on PathAcl and TimelineAcl.

title: Gate 3 T1: glob, AclBackend, MemoryAclBackend
description: Hand-rolled glob_matches helper, AclBackend sync trait, MemoryAclBackend (Arc<RwLock<HashMap>>), all ACL types (Permission, PathRole, TimelineRole, Accessor, PathAcl, TimelineAcl), AclStore, AccessDenied error variant, lib.rs wiring.

title: Gate 5 T4: T5 integration tests
description: Create tests/repo.rs with two T5 spec tests: (1) 1000 snapshot round-trip memory->disk->reload verifying all IDs and 10 tags, (2) materialize a 3-file nested snapshot, drop dest, re-materialize to new dest and verify contents match.

title: Gate 5 T3: materialize() function
description: Implement materialize(objects, snapshot_id, dest): fetch Snapshot, recursively walk Tree entries, write Blobs to dest directory. Async recursive helper uses Box::pin. Error on missing or wrong-type objects.

title: Gate 5 T2: Repository type and copy utilities
description: Add set_raw to RefStore (pub(crate)), create src/repo/mod.rs with copy_objects (async), copy_refs (sync), and Repository struct (memory/disk constructors + copy_to). Wire up pub mod repo in lib.rs.

title: Gate 5 T1: list() on StorageBackend
description: Add list() method to StorageBackend trait, implement in MemoryBackend (iterate HashMap) and DiskBackend (walk objects/ shards), expose as ObjectStore::list(). Required for copy_objects free fn in Task 2.

title: fix: tmp-shadow and create-overwrite bugs in refs module
Gate2 T7: public API + integration tests
description: Add refs re-exports to lib.rs and write spec T2 integration tests.

title: Gate2 T6: RefStore
description: Public API layer wrapping Box<dyn RefBackend> with type-safe tag and timeline operations.

title: Gate2 T5: DiskRefBackend
description: File-per-ref storage mirroring ref name as directory hierarchy. Postcard. Atomic write-then-rename.

title: Gate2 T4: RefBackend + MemoryRefBackend
description: Sync RefBackend trait and in-memory implementation with Arc<RwLock<HashMap>>.

title: Gate2 T3: Tag Timeline Ref types
description: Tag, Timeline, TimelinePolicy, and Ref enum with serde derives for postcard.

title: Gate2 T2: RefName
description: Validated hierarchical RefName struct with new(), as_str(), prefix(), Display.

title: Gate2 T1: scaffold + error variants
description: Add InvalidRefName and WrongRefKind to error.rs. Create src/refs/ module tree with stub files. Add pub mod refs to lib.rs.

title: Gate 1: public API + integration tests
description: Wire up lib.rs re-exports and write the spec T1 integration tests. This is the Gate 1 acceptance check — all tests here map directly to spec T1 requirements.

title: Gate 1: ObjectStore
description: The public API layer. Owns serialization, hashing, and idempotent deduplication. Wraps any StorageBackend via Box<dyn StorageBackend>.

title: Gate 1: DiskBackend
description: Disk-backed storage using file-per-object layout sharded by hash prefix. Always compresses with zstd level 3. Same StorageBackend contract as MemoryBackend.

title: Gate 1: StorageBackend trait + MemoryBackend
description: The async storage abstraction (trait object) and the in-memory implementation. MemoryBackend is used in tests and ephemeral agent workflows.

title: Gate 2: object types + codec
description: Blob, Tree, Snapshot, Object enum with serde derives, and the postcard codec layer. codec::serialize + deserialize + object_id are how ObjectStore computes content addresses.

title: Gate 1: ObjectId
description: 32-byte BLAKE3 content address. The identity of every object in the store. Includes Display (hex), serde derives, and Hash/Eq/Ord impls.

title: Gate 1: scaffold + error types
description: Initialize the bole library crate with Cargo.toml, all module stubs, and the crate-level Error enum. Foundation for every other task.

title: Wire MultiRecipientSecret into Object model + CLI
description: bole-21g delivered the multi-recipient envelope crypto primitive (MultiRecipientSecret: encrypt_for/decrypt/grant/revoke, per-actor DK wraps). Remaining integration: add an Object::MultiRecipientSecret variant (new variant, not a SecretV2 field change — postcard is not self-describing), store put/get helpers, per-actor key resolution in the CLI, and porcelain 'secret grant-actor <name> <actor>' / 'secret revoke-actor'. Also decide storage of actor->key_ref mapping. Depends on bole-21g.

title: Actor-scoped key wrapping for secrets
description: Today all cleared actors unwrap via the same master key. Extend envelope encryption so data keys can be wrapped per-actor (or per-label), rotation tied to labels/timelines. Design-level; larger than the others.

title: WS7 Part B: full doc repositioning (CLI.md, API.md, bole-cli README, skills)
description: Deferred from WS7 (bole-3hj). Complete the Part B narrative rewrite per spec 2026-06-29-ws7 section 3.4: docs/CLI.md mental model + linked-worktrees section, docs/API.md core-concepts + Access model subsection, bole-cli/README.md opening + worktree row, and skills/{claude-code/SKILL.md, codex/AGENTS.md, hermes/SKILL.md} description+opening+critical-facts (kept in sync). Lead with the access-control differentiator; mark WS1-6 realized (they shipped). README positioning + roadmap table already done in bole-3hj. Also the multi-actor worked example script (tests/example_multi_actor.sh).
close_reason: WS7 Part B doc repositioning complete. CLI.md (expanded mental model + linked-worktree hardening section), API.md (Core-concepts framing + Access model subsection), bole-cli/README (repositioned opening + Worktree row), skills claude-code/hermes/codex (new description + access-inside-repo addendum + --as/prune-repair critical facts; two SKILL.md kept identical). All lead with the access-control differentiator; WS1-6 framed as realized. Deferred: runnable multi-actor example script (needs aspirational acl/actor CLI verbs). Commit 9e47dfa.

title: Reconcile WS1 spec: document async PolicyHook::check deviation
description: WS1 implementation made PolicyHook::check async (via async_trait) because timeline fast-forward ancestry walks need ObjectStore::get().await. The WS1 spec (docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-29-ws1-access-policy-core.md) sketches it as sync. Update the spec text to match the shipped async signature so future readers aren't misled. Implementation plan already notes the deviation.
close_reason: WS1 spec PolicyHook::check sketch updated to the shipped async signature + rationale (async_trait; ancestry walks need get().await). Doc-only. Commit.

title: Clean up pre-existing unused-import warnings in src/repo/mod.rs
description: Pre-existing warnings (not from WS1/WS2) across the workspace: (1) src/repo/mod.rs:17 imports PathRole+Permission used only in #[cfg(test)] modules with local imports; (2) ObjectId imported unused in ~3 repo/mod.rs test-module use statements; (3) bole-cli field worktree_id never read. Clean these up for a warning-free 'cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets'.
close_reason: Cleared all pre-existing warnings: repo/mod.rs module-level PathRole/Permission + 3 unused ObjectId test imports removed; worktree_id marked allow(dead_code). cargo build --workspace is now warning-free; clippy clean; 23 test binaries green. Commit.

title: Skills: add EphemeralWorkspace note
description: The skills/ guides (claude-code, codex, hermes) already cover linked worktrees, policy enforcement, and glob semantics. Add a short note about the library's in-memory EphemeralWorkspace (Repository::memory() + ephemeral_workspace()) for in-process agents, pointing to docs/API.md.

title: Add agent skills for using the bole CLI
description: Create skills/ guides teaching agents to drive the bole CLI for version control: skills/claude-code/SKILL.md (YAML frontmatter), skills/codex/AGENTS.md (Codex format), skills/hermes/SKILL.md (frontmatter). Content covers the mental model, core workflow, command groups, and gotchas; links to docs/CLI.md and bole-cli/README.md.

title: Add MIT license
description: Add a LICENSE file (MIT, Leslie Russell, 2026), set license="MIT" in both Cargo.toml manifests, and update the README License section from TBD to MIT.

title: Docs: bole-cli/README.md
description: Add a crate-level README for bole-cli covering every command and flag with multiple worked workflow examples.

title: Docs: CLI reference + README update
description: Add docs/CLI.md command reference for the bole binary and update README to reflect that a CLI now ships (correcting the 'not a CLI' line) with a CLI quick start.

title: CLI: plumbing (object/ref/store) + repo info
description: Add low-level plumbing: object (list/get/type/cat/put-blob), ref (list/get/delete), store (stats/fsck), and repo info. Wraps ObjectStore/RefStore directly. No src/ changes.

title: G7: project_to_git doesn't set HEAD to a projected branch
description: gix::init_bare sets HEAD to gix default branch name. If the bole repo's only timeline isn't named that default, a bare 'git log' reports 'no commits' even though refs/heads/<name> is valid. project_to_git should write HEAD to point at one of the projected branches.

title: Strengthen nonce uniqueness test to assert distinct ObjectIds
description: test two_encryptions_have_different_nonces in src/object/secret.rs only asserts nonce inequality at the struct level. The security-relevant property is that two put_secret calls with identical plaintext produce distinct ObjectIds (proving no equality leakage through the content-addressed store). Add a store-level test asserting distinct IDs. Discovered during Gate 4 final whole-branch review.

title: check_merge returns Allowed for missing/non-snapshot source ref
description: When the source ref is missing or its head is not a Snapshot object, check_merge returns MergeCheck::Allowed rather than Rejected or an error. For a security gate this may be wrong — a non-resolvable source arguably warrants rejection.

title: DiskAclBackend: sanitize should escape % to prevent ACL collision
description: sanitize() escapes / and * but not %. Globs 'a/b' and 'a%2Fb' both map to filename 'a%2Fb', causing a silent ACL overwrite. Fix: escape % first (to %25), then / and *.

title: DiskBackend::list swallows shard open errors
description: In src/store/disk.rs DiskBackend::list(), shard-directory open failures are silently swallowed (Err(_) => continue) while entry enumeration errors propagate via ?. If a shard is unreadable due to permissions, copy_to would produce a silently incomplete copy. Recommend distinguishing NotFound (skip) from other errors (propagate), mirroring how delete/the outer read_dir handle it.

title: Untagged new-code blocks in src/repo/mod.rs
description: The 'use' imports + struct Repository block and the #[cfg(test)] mod tests block in src/repo/mod.rs are missing // bole-1vi bead comments. Per-task reviews fixed similar issues in set_raw and materialize test block but these were missed. Add // bole-1vi before each untagged contiguous block.

title: MemoryBackend: use ObjectId as HashMap key instead of [u8;32]
description: MemoryBackend stores Bytes keyed by [u8; 32] but ObjectId already derives Hash + Eq and can be used directly. Using the raw byte array discards the type-safety invariant. Replace HashMap<[u8;32], Bytes> with HashMap<ObjectId, Bytes>.

title: ObjectId: add FromStr impl (parse hex string)
description: Display emits 64-char lowercase hex but there is no FromStr. The CLI will need to parse ObjectIds from user input and config. Implement FromStr that parses a 64-char hex string back into ObjectId.

title: t6_agent_capability_enforced: add intent comment
description: Add a comment in the t6_agent_capability_enforced test explaining that passing the same snapshot as the current head is intentional. Guards against a future same-head short-circuit optimization in advance_timeline silently skipping path-level enforcement.

title: check_merge: use Accessor::privileged() instead of inline
description: Refactor the ad-hoc Accessor construction inside check_merge (bole-hc1 block) to use the new Accessor::privileged() constructor added in Gate 6. Currently builds inline with with_path_role, which is functionally identical but inconsistent with the new helper.

title: merge tests: add dedicated lca_diamond test
description: Add a test named lca_diamond that explicitly covers the diamond DAG topology (base → L/R → tip). The topology is currently tested indirectly but has no explicitly named test.

title: find_common_ancestor: remove dead overlap guards
description: Remove the two permanently-false 'immediate overlap' guard checks in find_common_ancestor (visited_b.contains(&a) and visited_a.contains(&b) immediately after initial setup). At that point visited_a={a}, visited_b={b}, and a!=b is already guaranteed by the early return.

title: three_way_diff: replace unreachable dead arm
description: Replace (_, None, None) => {} in three_way_diff match with unreachable!() to make the dead arm explicit. The arm can never fire because the early our==thr guard already exits when both are None.

title: check_merge: hoist timeline_is_protected(dest) out of per-path loop
description: timeline_is_protected(dest) returns a loop-invariant value but is called inside the per-ACL loop via path_is_protected call chain. Hoist it before the loop.

title: copy_objects raw-byte note
description: copy_objects in src/repo/mod.rs decodes via get() then re-encodes via put() rather than raw byte copy. Functionally correct today (postcard is deterministic, content-addressed ids stable). Add a comment documenting the determinism assumption. If codec versioning ever changes this approach needs revisiting.

title: ObjectId::from_bytes: rename to clarify it hashes input
description: ObjectId::from_bytes(data: &[u8]) computes a BLAKE3 hash of arbitrary data, but the name implies constructing from the ObjectId's own byte representation (which is ObjectId::new). Consider renaming to hash(), from_data(), or from_content() to avoid confusion. ObjectId::new([u8;32]) is already the right name for the literal-bytes constructor.

title: Integration tests: run T1 scenarios against DiskBackend
description: tests/object_store.rs T1 tests (immutability, dedup, parent preservation, tree round-trip) only use MemoryBackend. The shared helper pattern in the spec should be used so the same scenarios run against DiskBackend too. Low risk since backend is bytes-opaque, but closes the gap.
